note: Official expectation model only - live integration NOT executed and NO compatibility/certification is claimed.
authMethod: OAuth2. Server-to-server: JWT bearer flow (signed assertion to /services/oauth2/token) or client-credentials flow; interactive: web-server flow. Access token sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'; calls go to the returned instance_url.

expectedRequestShape.create: POST /sobjects/Account  header Authorization: Bearer <token>, Content-Type: application/json  body { "Name": "Acme" }
expectedRequestShape.requirement: Connected App with the right OAuth scopes (api, refresh_token); the integration user's profile/permission set grants object + field access.

expectedFailureResponseShape.http: 401 INVALID_SESSION_ID (expired token)  OR  403 REQUEST_LIMIT_EXCEEDED (API limits)  OR  400 MALFORMED_QUERY / INVALID_FIELD  OR  404 NOT_FOUND

commonIntegrationFailure: 401 INVALID_SESSION_ID when the access token expired and was not refreshed; 400 INVALID_FIELD/MALFORMED_QUERY when SOQL references a field the user cannot see or a typo; 403 REQUEST_LIMIT_EXCEEDED when the org's 24h API call quota is exhausted; using a stale instance_url after a migration.
diagnosticCommand: GET /services/data/ to list supported API versions; GET /services/data/v60.0/limits to inspect remaining API calls; re-run the OAuth token request and use the returned instance_url verbatim.
preventionRule: Refresh the OAuth access token on 401 and retry once; always call the instance_url returned by the token response; validate SOQL fields against the object describe; monitor /limits to stay under the API quota; pin a tested API version.
